title: [BUG] Deleting a `ovh_cloud_project_storage` can return Internal Server Errors
author: jriouovh
body:
### Describe the bug

When deleting an `ovh_cloud_project_storage` resource with a lot of objects, the API can intermittently returns Internal Server Errors. In that case, retrying solves the issue. It happened multiple times in the DE region.

### Terraform Version

1.15.3

### OVH Terraform Provider Version

2.13.1

### Affected Resource(s)

- ovh_cloud_project_storage

### Terraform Configuration Files

```hcl
resource "ovh_cloud_project_storage" "name" {
  service_name = var.service_name
  region_name  = var.region_name
  name         = var.name
}
```

### Panic Output

```
ovh_cloud_project_storage.control_plane: Still destroying... [id=ID/REGION/NAME, 69m41s elapsed]
╷
│ Error: Error deleting bucket: NAME
│
│ error calling POST
│ /cloud/project/ID/region/REGION/storage/NAME/bulkDeleteObjects:
│ OVHcloud API error (status code 500): Server::InternalServerError:
│ "Internal server error" (X-OVH-Query-Id:
│ EU.ext-REQUEST_ID)
╵
```

### Expected Behavior

The provider should retry on Internal Server Errors.

### Actual Behavior

The plan failed.

### Steps to Reproduce

Please list the steps required to reproduce the issue, for example:
1. `terraform apply` to create the bucket in the DE region
2. Generate millions of objects
3. `terraform destroy` to drop the bucket

title: [FEATURE] Support delete timeout for `ovh_cloud_project_storage`
author: jriouovh
body:
### Description

When deleting a `ovh_cloud_project_storage` (aka s3 bucket) with a lot of objects, it can take a very long time. By default, the plan times out at 2 hours, which may not be enough for everyone:

```
ovh_cloud_project_storage.control_plane: Still destroying... [id=ID/REGION/NAME, 119m30s elapsed]
ovh_cloud_project_storage.control_plane: Still destroying... [id=ID/REGION/NAME, 119m40s elapsed]

Interrupt received.
Please wait for Terraform to exit or data loss may occur.
Gracefully shutting down...

Stopping operation...
╷
│ Error: Error deleting bucket: NAME
│ 
│ error calling POST
│ /cloud/project/ID/region/REGION/storage/NAME/bulkDeleteObjects:
│ Post
│ "https://ca.api.ovh.com/1.0/cloud/project/ID/region/REGION/storageNAME/bulkDeleteObjects":
│ context canceled
╵
```

### Affected Resource(s) and/or Data Source(s)

* ovh_2.13.1

### Potential Terraform Configuration

Add support for 

```
resource "ovh_cloud_project_storage" "name" {
  service_name = var.service_name
  region_name  = var.region_name
  name         = var.name

  # Each object must be deleted individually before being able to delete the bucket.
  # This process can take a very long time. The default delete of the provider is 2h.
  timeouts {
    delete = "24h"
  }
}
```

title: [BUG] `OVHcloud API error (status code 404): Client::NotFound: \"This service does not exist\"` when creating public cloud project
author: tpulliam-nxt
body:
### Describe the bug
I am trying to create a new Public Cloud Project `ovh_cloud_project`.

I am able to access the project and create resources within it in the Web UI
(e.g. gateways and private networks).

Terraform, however, continues polling for project availability and
eventually fails with:

404 Client::NotFound: "This service does not exist"

After the failed apply, the resource becomes tainted and Terraform
subsequently believes the project has been deleted.


```bash
$ terraform apply
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_me.myaccount: Reading...
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_me.myaccount: Read complete after 1s [id=brxxxxxx-ovh]
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart.mycart: Reading...
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart.mycart: Read complete after 0s [id=xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxx]
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart_product_plan.cloud: Reading...
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart_product_plan.cloud: Read complete after 3s [id=project.2018]

Terraform used the selected providers to generate the following execution plan. Resource actions are indicated with the following symbols:
  + create

Terraform will perform the following actions:

  # module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project will be created
  + resource "ovh_cloud_project" "project" {
      + access              = (known after apply)
      + deletion_protection = false
      + description         = "my-ovh-project"
      + id                  = (known after apply)
      + ovh_subsidiary      = "DE"
      + project_id          = (known after apply)
      + project_name        = (known after apply)
      + status              = (known after apply)
      + urn                 = (known after apply)

      + order (known after apply)

      + plan {
          + duration     = "P1M"
          + plan_code    = "project.2018"
          + pricing_mode = "default"
        }
    }

...
[SNIP]
...

module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project: Still creating... [10m10s elapsed]
module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project: Still creating... [10m20s elapsed]
module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project: Still creating... [10m30s elapsed]
╷
│ Error: waiting for cloud project xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx to become available: "OVHcloud API error (status code 404): Client::NotFound: \"This service does not exist\" (X-OVH-Query-Id: EU.ext-4.xxxxxxxx.xxxxxx.xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx)"
│ 
│   with module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project,
│   on ../modules/project/main.tf line 16, in resource "ovh_cloud_project" "project":
│   16: resource "ovh_cloud_project" "project" {
```

### Terraform / OVH Terraform Provider Version

```bash
$ terraform -v
Terraform v1.15.7
on linux_amd64
+ provider registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/random v3.9.0
+ provider registry.terraform.io/ovh/ovh v2.15.0

$ terraform providers

Providers required by configuration:
.
├── provider[registry.terraform.io/ovh/ovh] >= 2.15.0
├── provider[registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/random] >= 3.9.0
└── module.ovh_project
    ├── provider[registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/random] >= 3.9.0
    └── provider[registry.terraform.io/ovh/ovh] >= 2.15.0

Providers required by state:

    provider[registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/random]

    provider[registry.terraform.io/ovh/ovh]
```

### Affected Resource(s)
Output of `terraform state list`:

- `module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_me.myaccount`
- `module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart.mycart`
- `module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart_product_plan.cloud`
- `module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project`
- `module.ovh_project["mgm"].random_string.suffix`

### Terraform Configuration Files

```hcl
# main.tf
data "ovh_me" "myaccount" {}


data "ovh_order_cart" "mycart" {
  ovh_subsidiary = data.ovh_me.myaccount.ovh_subsidiary
}

data "ovh_order_cart_product_plan" "cloud" {
  cart_id        = data.ovh_order_cart.mycart.id
  price_capacity = "renew"
  product        = "cloud"
  plan_code      = "project.2018"
  # plan_code    = "project" # when running in the US
}

resource "ovh_cloud_project" "project" {
  ovh_subsidiary = data.ovh_me.myaccount.ovh_subsidiary
  description = "${var.name}"
  

  plan {
    duration     = data.ovh_order_cart_product_plan.cloud.selected_price[0].duration
    plan_code    = data.ovh_order_cart_product_plan.cloud.plan_code
    pricing_mode = data.ovh_order_cart_product_plan.cloud.selected_price[0].pricing_mode
  }
}

resource "ovh_iam_resource_tags" "project_tags" {
  urn = ovh_cloud_project.project.urn

  tags = var.tags
}
```

### Debug Output

N/A

### Panic Output

N/A

### Expected Behavior

The project should be created.

### Actual Behavior

Resource creation starts successfully.
Order is created successfully.
After ~10m the provider fails while waiting for project availability:

404 Client::NotFound: "This service does not exist"

Project is visible in the web UI, and I can even create a gateway / private network in the project. So it appears to be functional in the UI.

The Terraform resource is in a tainted state:

```bash
$ terraform state show 'module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_proje
ct.project'
# module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project: (tainted)
resource "ovh_cloud_project" "project" {
    deletion_protection = false
    description         = "my-ovh-project"
    id                  = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
    ovh_subsidiary      = "DE"
    project_id          = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"

    order {
        date            = "2026-07-09T15:08:30+02:00"
        details         = [
            {
                description     = "/cloud project activation"
                domain          = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
                order_detail_id = xxxxxxxx
                quantity        = "1"
            },
            {
                description     = "Public Cloud Project rental for 1 month"
                domain          = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
                order_detail_id = xxxxxxxx
                quantity        = "1"
            },
            {
                description     = "Public Cloud Project rental for 1 month"
                domain          = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
                order_detail_id = xxxxxxxxx
                quantity        = "1"
            },
        ]
        expiration_date = "2026-07-23T23:29:59+02:00"
        order_id        = xxxxxxxx
    }

    plan {
        catalog_name = null
        duration     = "P1M"
        plan_code    = "project.2018"
        pricing_mode = "default"
    }
}
```

Because of this tainted state, the next `terraform plan` marks the project for deletion:

```bash
$ terraform plan
module.ovh_project["mgm"].random_string.suffix: Refreshing state... [id=5qt6kv7c2w2krnd1]
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_me.myaccount: Reading...
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_me.myaccount: Read complete after 1s [id=brxxxxxxx-ovh]
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart.mycart: Reading...
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart.mycart: Read complete after 1s [id=xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx]
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart_product_plan.cloud: Reading...
module.ovh_project["mgm"].data.ovh_order_cart_product_plan.cloud: Read complete after 2s [id=project.2018]
module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project: Refreshing state... [id=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx]

Note: Objects have changed outside of Terraform

Terraform detected the following changes made outside of Terraform since the last "terraform apply" which may
have affected this plan:

  # module.ovh_project["mgm"].ovh_cloud_project.project has been deleted
  - resource "ovh_cloud_project" "project" {
        id                  = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
      - project_id          = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx" -> null
        # (3 unchanged attributes hidden)

        # (2 unchanged blocks hidden)
    }
```

### Steps to Reproduce

Please list the steps required to reproduce the issue, for example:
1. `terraform plan`
2.  `terraform apply`

### References

N/A

### Additional context

The Project appears to be otherwise functional in the web UI.

title: [FEATURE] `ovh_cloud_project_region_network` expose subnet id
author: tpulliam-nxt
body:
### Description

I am referencing this previous bug fix. https://github.com/ovh/terraform-provider-ovh/issues/802

It seems [ovh_cloud_project_region_network ](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/resources/cloud_project_region_network) will be replacing [ovh_cloud_project_network_private](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/resources/cloud_project_network_private).

Because of this `ovh_cloud_project_region_network` defines the subnet in-line, and it is not possible to obtain the subnet id to be passed to [ovh_cloud_project_gateway](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/resources/cloud_project_gateway)

### Affected Resource(s) and/or Data Source(s)

* ovh_cloud_project_region_network
* ovh_cloud_project_gateway

### Potential Terraform Configuration

Potential solution could look like:

```HCL
resource "ovh_cloud_project_network_private_subnet_v2" "subnet" {
  service_name      = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
  network_id        = "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"
  name              = "my_private_subnet"
  region            = "XXX1"
  dns_nameservers   = ["1.1.1.1"]
  cidr              = "192.168.168.0/24"
  dhcp              = true
  enable_gateway_ip = true
  use_default_public_dns_resolver = false
}

resource "ovh_cloud_project_region_network" "net" {
   service_name = "XXXXXX"
   region_name  = "EU-SOUTH-LZ-MAD-A"
   name         = "Madrid Network"
   subnet_id    = ovh_cloud_project_network_private_subnet_v2.subnet.id
}

resource "ovh_cloud_project_gateway" "gateway" {
  service_name = ovh_cloud_project_network_private.mypriv.service_name
  name         = "my-gateway"
  model        = "s"
  region       = ovh_cloud_project_network_private_subnet_v2.subnet.region
  network_id   = tolist(ovh_cloud_project_network_private.net.regions_attributes[*].openstackid)[0]
  subnet_id    = ovh_cloud_project_network_private_subnet_v2.subnet.id
}
```

title: [BUG] Kafka topic properties should update in place
author: xbrassoud-ovk
body:
### Describe the bug

Hi the OVH Terraform developers.

Inconsistent behavior from the OVH Manager, update Kafka topic properties (`min_insync_replicas`, `partitions`, `replication`, `retention_bytes`, `retention_hours`) currently **destroy and recreate** the topic instead of **update in place**. The note "(Optional, Forces new resource)" in the [documentation](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/resources/cloud_project_database_kafka_topic#min_insync_replicas-1) also contradicts the OVH Manager behavior.

### Terraform Version

OpenTofu v1.12.3

### OVH Terraform Provider Version

``` sh
$ tofu init

Initializing the backend...

Initializing provider plugins...
- Reusing previous version of hashicorp/vault from the dependency lock file
- Reusing previous version of ovh/ovh from the dependency lock file
- Using previously-installed ovh/ovh v2.14.0
```

### Affected Resource(s)

- ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic

### Terraform Configuration Files

```hcl
resource "ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic" "topic" {
  service_name        = data.ovh_cloud_project_database.kafka.service_name
  cluster_id          = data.ovh_cloud_project_database.kafka.id
  name                = "mytopic"
  partitions          =  1 # -> 12
}
```

### Debug Output

NC

### Panic Output

NC

### Expected Behavior

Update Kafka topic properties (`min_insync_replicas`, `partitions`, `replication`, `retention_bytes`, `retention_hours`) should **update in place**.

### Actual Behavior

Update Kafka topic properties (`min_insync_replicas`, `partitions`, `replication`, `retention_bytes`, `retention_hours`) currently **destroy and recreate** the topic.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. `terraform plan`
2. `terraform apply`

### References

Documentation :
- https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/resources/cloud_project_database_kafka_topic

### Additional context

PLAN:

``` bash
OpenTofu used the selected providers to generate the following execution
plan. Resource actions are indicated with the following symbols:
  ~ update in-place (current -> planned)
-/+ destroy and then create replacement

OpenTofu will perform the following actions:

  # module.ovh-managed-kafka.ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic.topic["mytopic"] must be replaced
-/+ resource "ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic" "topic" {
      ~ id                  = "xxxxxxx-xxxxxxx-xxxxxxxx-xxxxxxx" -> (known after apply)
      ~ min_insync_replicas = 1 -> (known after apply)
        name                = "mytopic"
      ~ partitions          = 1 -> 12 # forces replacement
      ~ replication         = 2 -> (known after apply)
      ~ retention_bytes     = -1 -> (known after apply)
      ~ retention_hours     = 168 -> (known after apply)
        # (2 unchanged attributes hidden)
    }

Plan: 1 to add, 0 to change, 1 to destroy.
```

APPLY:

``` bash
tofu apply
Initializing the backend...
Successfully configured the backend "http"! OpenTofu will automatically
use this backend unless the backend configuration changes.
Initializing modules...
Initializing provider plugins...
- Reusing previous version of hashicorp/vault from the dependency lock file
- Reusing previous version of ovh/ovh from the dependency lock file
- Using previously-installed ovh/ovh v2.14.0
OpenTofu has been successfully initialized!
module.ovh-managed-kafka.ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic.topic["mytopic"]: Destroying... [id=xxxxxxx-xxxxxxx-xxxxxxxx-xxxxxxx]
module.ovh-managed-kafka.ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic.topic["mytopic"]: Still destroying... [id=xxxxxxx-xxxxxxx-xxxxxxxx-xxxxxxx, 10s elapsed]
module.ovh-managed-kafka.ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic.topic["mytopic"]: Destruction complete after 15s
module.ovh-managed-kafka.ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic.topic["mytopic"]: Creating...
module.ovh-managed-kafka.ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic.topic["mytopic"]: Still creating... [10s elapsed]
module.ovh-managed-kafka.ovh_cloud_project_database_kafka_topic.topic["mytopic"]: Creation complete after 14s [id=yyyyyyyy-yyyyyyy-yyyyyyyy-yyyyyyyyyy]
```

title: [BUG] ovh_cloud_project_kube loadBalancersSubnetId parameter error
author: aperigault
body:
### Describe the bug

When I remove the previously defined loadBalancersSubnetId parameter from my ovh_cloud_project_kube configuration I got an error.

### Terraform Version
OpenTofu v1.12.0

### OVH Terraform Provider Version

v2.14.0

### Affected Resource(s)

- ovh_cloud_project_kube

### Debug Output

`Error: OVHcloud API error (status code 400): Client::BadRequest: "[loadBalancersSubnetId] Given data () is not valid for type uuid"`

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create an ovh_cloud_project_kube ressource with loadBalancersSubnetId parameter.
2. Remove loadBalancersSubnetId parameter
3. terraform apply

title: [New Resource] `ovh_cloud_project_storage_object`
author: anthonyroussel
body:
### Description

Introduce a Terraform resource that allows uploading objects directly to an S3-compatible bucket, similar to the [`aws_s3_object` resource](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/-/aws/latest/docs/resources/s3_object) provided by the AWS provider.

Currently, `ovh_cloud_project_storage_object` is only available as a data source, making it possible to read object metadata but not to create, update, or manage objects within a bucket through Terraform.

### Requested Resource(s) and/or Data Source(s)

* `ovh_cloud_project_storage_object`

### Potential Terraform Configuration

```hcl
resource "ovh_cloud_project_storage_object" "my_object" {
  service_name = var.service_name
  region_name  = var.region
  name         = ovh_cloud_project_storage.bucket.name

  key          = "index.html"
  content      = file("${path.module}/files/index.html")
  content_type = "text/html"

  acl           = "public-read"
  storage_class = "STANDARD"
}
```

### Use case

This resource would enable users to manage bucket contents declaratively with Terraform, including uploading static website assets, configuration files, and other objects as part of their infrastructure provisioning workflow, without relying on external scripts or additional providers.

**Note:** While objects can be uploaded using the S3 `PutObject` API, it is not currently integrated with OVH IAM, which prevents its use via the ovh Terraform provider natively at this time (see https://github.com/ovh/management-security-operations-roadmap/issues/10)

title: [DOCS] Some resources appear in the wrong sidebar sections
author: anthonyroussel
body:
### Documentation link

https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs

### Description

While browsing the updated documentation, I noticed a few resources that seem to be categorized under the wrong sidebar section:

* Resource `ovh_cloud_project_file_storage_share` is listed under **Cloud Project** section, but would probably fit better under **File Storage** section
* Resource `ovh_cloud_project_file_storage_share_network`, same as above
* Resource `ovh_cloud_project_volume` is listed under **Cloud Project** section, but would probably fit better under **Block Storage** section for consistency with the matching data source, which is already categorized there
* Resource `ovh_cloud_project_volume_backup`, same as above
* Datasource `ovh_cloud_project_flavor` and `ovh_cloud_project_flavors` are listed under **Cloud Project** section, but would probably fit better under **Instances** section
* Datasource `ovh_cloud_project_region` and `ovh_cloud_project_regions` are listed under **Cloud Project** section, but would probably fit better under **Instances** section

Nothing blocking, but these resources can be a bit harder to find when browsing the documentation. Thanks!

title: [New Resource] `ovh_cloud_project_storage_logging`
author: anthonyroussel
body:
### Description
We can create and manage buckets with `ovh_cloud_project_storage`, but there's no way to enable or configure [S3 server access logging](https://docs.ovhcloud.com/en/guides/storage-and-backup/object-storage/s3-server-access-logging) on a bucket with the OVH Terraform provider.

At this time, we have to mix in the AWS provider and use its [aws_s3_bucket_logging](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/resources/s3_bucket_logging) resource (pointed at the OVHcloud S3 endpoint) to configure it. Having it natively in the OVH provider would avoid pulling in a second provider just for this.

### Requested Resource(s) and/or Data Source(s)

* `ovh_cloud_project_storage_logging`

### Potential Terraform Configuration

```hcl
resource "ovh_cloud_project_storage" "documents" {
  service_name = "<public cloud project ID>"
  region_name  = "EU-WEST-PAR"
  name         = "documents"
}

resource "ovh_cloud_project_storage" "bucket_access_logs" {
  service_name = "<public cloud project ID>"
  region_name  = "EU-WEST-PAR"
  name         = "access-logs"
}

resource "ovh_cloud_project_storage_logging" "documents" {
  service_name  = "<public cloud project ID>"
  region_name   = "EU-WEST-PAR"
  bucket        = ovh_cloud_project_storage.documents.name
  target_bucket = ovh_cloud_project_storage.bucket_access_logs.name
  target_prefix = "access-logs/${ovh_cloud_project_storage.documents.name}"
}
```

title: [New Resource] `ovh_cloud_project_storage_acl`

title: [DOCS] Inaccurate "Attributes Reference" for `ovh_cloud_project_instances` data source
author: abstrask
body:
### Documentation link

<https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/data-sources/cloud_project_instances>

### Description

Under "[Attributes Reference](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/data-sources/cloud_project_instances#attributes-reference)", all properties under `instances` appear to be flat.

Instead, `ip` and `version` should appear under `addresses`. And `id` (Volume id) should appear under `attached_volumes`.

This is consistent with how the [data source for a single instance](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/data-sources/cloud_project_instance#attributes-reference) is documented.

I wanted to create a PR to fix this, but the [source Markdown file](https://github.com/ovh/terraform-provider-ovh/blob/master/docs/data-sources/cloud_project_instances.md) seems to be correct.

Some kind of rendering issue?

title: [BUG] Immediate "timeout while waiting kube ... to be READY" modifying MKS `customization_apiserver`
author: abstrask
body:
### Describe the bug
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

### Terraform Version

```
$ tofu -version

OpenTofu v1.11.6
on darwin_arm64
...
+ provider registry.opentofu.org/ovh/ovh v2.13.1
```

### OVH Terraform Provider Version

```
+ provider registry.opentofu.org/ovh/ovh v2.13.1
```

### Affected Resource(s)

Please list the resources as a list, for example:
- ovh_cloud_project_kube

### Terraform Configuration Files

```hcl
resource "ovh_cloud_project_kube" "cluster" {
  service_name       = var.project_id
  name               = "${var.env}-cluster"
  region             = "EU-WEST-PAR"
  version            = var.kubernetes_version
  plan               = "standard"
  private_network_id = tolist(ovh_cloud_project_network_private.cluster_network.regions_attributes[*].openstackid)[0]
  nodes_subnet_id    = ovh_cloud_project_network_private_subnet.cluster_subnet.id
  customization_apiserver {
    admissionplugins {
      enabled  = ["NodeRestriction"]
      disabled = ["AlwaysPullImages"]
    }
  }
  depends_on = [ovh_cloud_project_gateway.cluster_gateway]
}
```

### Debug Output

I'm not able to share the full, or unredacted debug log, but this appears to be the relevant series of API requests and responses:

<https://gist.github.com/abstrask/a58b3e6cd428f6365d7ab6b4aba7a895>

I hope this will do.

### Expected Behavior

Plan applied without errors.

### Actual Behavior

Apply almost immediately times out (<10s), despite default [`update` timeout](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/ovh/ovh/latest/docs/resources/cloud_project_kube#timeouts) is supposedly 45m:

```
11:03:00.470 STDOUT tofu:   Enter a value:
yes
11:03:04.986 STDOUT tofu: ovh_cloud_project_kube.cluster: Modifying... [id=019e3aef-f972-71fc-ba47-297ae621f03f]
11:03:11.288 STDERR tofu:
│ Error: timeout while waiting kube 019c09c5-9276-7e64-9374-edd0dcd32576 to be READY: unexpected state 'UPDATING', wanted target 'READY'. last error: %!s(<nil>)
│
│   with ovh_cloud_project_kube.cluster,
│   on kubernetes_cluster.tf line 32, in resource "ovh_cloud_project_kube" "cluster":
│   32: resource "ovh_cloud_project_kube" "cluster" {
```

Running `plan` immediately after reveals no changes, so the configuration change is actually applied:

```
11:03:26.883 STDOUT tofu: random_password.external_secrets_user_password: Refreshing state... [id=none]
...
11:03:31.399 STDOUT tofu: No changes. Your infrastructure matches the configuration.
```

### Steps to Reproduce

Please list the steps required to reproduce the issue, for example:
1. On an existing MKS resource, change the `customization_apiserver.admissionplugins`, e.g. switch between:

```hcl
  customization_apiserver {
    admissionplugins {
      enabled  = ["NodeRestriction", "AlwaysPullImages"]
      disabled = []
    }
```

And:

```hcl
  customization_apiserver {
    admissionplugins {
      enabled  = ["NodeRestriction"]
      disabled = ["AlwaysPullImages"]
    }
```

2. Apply plan
3. Immediate timeout

### Additional context

Using OpenTofu with Terragrunt.

title: [BUG] Database plan upgrade should update in place
author: xbrassoud-ovk
body:
### Describe the bug

Hi the OVH Terraform developers.

Inconsistent behavior from the OVH Manager, perform an upgrade plan on a database currently **destroy and recreate** instead of **update in place**.

### Terraform Version

OpenTofu v1.12.1

### OVH Terraform Provider Version

``` sh
$ tofu init

Initializing the backend...

Initializing provider plugins...
- Reusing previous version of hashicorp/vault from the dependency lock file
- Reusing previous version of ovh/ovh from the dependency lock file
- Using previously-installed ovh/ovh v2.13.1
```

### Affected Resource(s)

Please list the resources as a list, for example:
- ovh_cloud_project_database

### Terraform Configuration Files

```hcl
locals {
  plan_nodes = {
    essential  = 1
    business   = 2
    production = 2
  }
}

resource "ovh_cloud_project_database" "ovh-managed-postgresql" {
  service_name           = var.ovh_public_cloud_project_id
  description            = "pgsql-01-eu-ovh-test"
  engine                 = "postgresql"
  version                = "15"
  flavor                 = "db1-4"
  plan                   = "essential" # -> "business"
  disk_size              = 80

  dynamic "nodes" {
    for_each = range(local.plan_nodes["essential"]) # -> "business"
    content {
      region     = "GRA"
    }
  }
  ip_restrictions {
      ip          = "0.0.0.0/0"
  }
}
```

### Debug Output

NC

### Panic Output

NC

### Expected Behavior

Perform an upgrade plan on a database should **update in place**.

### Actual Behavior

Perform an upgrade plan on a database currently **destroy and recreate**.

### Steps to Reproduce

Please list the steps required to reproduce the issue, for example:
1. `terraform plan`
2. `terraform apply`

### References

NC

### Additional context

PLAN:

``` bash
OpenTofu used the selected providers to generate the following execution
plan. Resource actions are indicated with the following symbols:
  ~ update in-place (current -> planned)
-/+ destroy and then create replacement

OpenTofu will perform the following actions:

  # module.ovh-managed-postgresql.ovh_cloud_project_database.ovh-managed-postgresql must be replaced
-/+ resource "ovh_cloud_project_database" "ovh-managed-postgresql" {
      ~ advanced_configuration  = {
          - "pg.io_combine_limit"                         = "16"
          - "pg.io_max_combine_limit"                     = "16"
          - "pg.io_max_concurrency"                       = "-1"
          - "pg.io_method"                                = "worker"
          - "pg.io_workers"                               = "3"
          - "pg.password_encryption"                      = "scram-sha-256"
          - "pglookout.max_failover_replication_time_lag" = "60"
        } -> (known after apply)
      ~ backup_regions          = [
          - "GRA",
          - "SBG",
        ] -> (known after apply)
      ~ backup_time             = "13:23:00" -> (known after apply)
      ~ created_at              = "2026-06-02T11:17:27.344727+02:00" -> (known after apply)
      - deletion_protection     = false -> null
      ~ disk_type               = "high-speed" -> (known after apply)
      ~ endpoints               = [
          - {
              - component = "postgresql"
              - domain    = "postgresql-zzzzzzzzzz-zzzzzzzzz.database.cloud.ovh.net"
              - path      = ""
              - port      = 20184
              - scheme    = "postgres"
              - ssl       = true
              - ssl_mode  = "require"
              - uri       = "postgres://<username>:<password>@postgresql-zzzzzzzzzz-zzzzzzzzz.database.cloud.ovh.net:20184/defaultdb?sslmode=require"
            },
        ] -> (known after apply)
      ~ id                      = "xxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxx-xxxxxxx" -> (known after apply)
      - kafka_rest_api          = false -> null
      - kafka_schema_registry   = false -> null
      ~ maintenance_time        = "07:47:00" -> (known after apply)
      ~ network_type            = "public" -> (known after apply)
      - opensearch_acls_enabled = false -> null
      ~ plan                    = "essential" -> "business"
      ~ status                  = "READY" -> (known after apply)
        # (6 unchanged attributes hidden)

      + nodes {
          + region = "GRA" # forces replacement
        }

        # (2 unchanged blocks hidden)
    }

  # module.ovh-managed-postgresql.ovh_cloud_project_database_database.ovh-managed-postgresql-database["test-db"] must be replaced
-/+ resource "ovh_cloud_project_database_database" "ovh-managed-postgresql-database" {
      ~ cluster_id   = "xxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxx-xxxxxxx" # forces replacement -> (known after apply) # forces replacement
      ~ default      = false -> (known after apply)
      ~ id           = "yyyyyyyyy-yyyyyyy-yyyyyyy" -> (known after apply)
        name         = "test-db"
        # (2 unchanged attributes hidden)
    }

Plan: 2 to add, 0 to change, 2 to destroy.
```

APPLY:

``` bash
tofu apply
Initializing the backend...
Successfully configured the backend "http"! OpenTofu will automatically
use this backend unless the backend configuration changes.
Initializing modules...
Initializing provider plugins...
- Reusing previous version of hashicorp/vault from the dependency lock file

module.ovh-managed-postgresql.ovh_cloud_project_database_database.ovh-managed-postgresql-database["test-db"]: Destruction complete after 24s
```

title: [BUG] Rescue SSH key breaks the `ovh_dedicated_server` installation
author: jouir
body:
### Describe the bug

When there is an issue with a dedicated server, we reboot server host in rescue mode with a SSH key. When the debug is finished, sometimes we install the server with the provider. The rescue ssh key is not defined in the provider because it doesn't matter and it pollutes the code base. The installation fails with the provider because the rescue SSH key is returned by the API and the provider doesn't know what to do about it. But the server is successfully installed. The next plan will reinstall the server.

### Terraform Version

```
Terraform v1.15.5
on linux_amd64
```

### OVH Terraform Provider Version

Commit e857753b.

### Affected Resource(s)

- ovh_dedicated_server


### Terraform Configuration Files

```hcl
terraform {
  required_providers {
    ovh = {
      source = "ovh/ovh"
    }
  }
}

data "ovh_me" "account" {}

resource "ovh_dedicated_server" "server" {
  ovh_subsidiary = data.ovh_me.account.ovh_subsidiary
  service_name   = "*****"
  os             = "debian13_64"
}
```

### Output

```
$ terraform apply

│ ****").

```

### Expected Behavior

The rescue SSH key should be ignored or removed. The next plan does nothing.

### Actual Behavior

The provider crashed. The next plan reinstall the server.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. On the manager, select **Boot**, **Edit**, **Boot in rescue mode**, **Authentication via SSH key**
2. Reboot hard
3. Wait for rescue to be effective
4. `terraform apply`

### Additional context

Payload returned by the API before install:

`GET /dedicated/server/ns***`:

```json
{
  "name": "****",
  "rescueSshKey": "ssh-rsa ***"
}
```

title: [META] Comprehensive VPS API coverage — 20 resources + 40 data sources + bug fixes
author: 88plug
body:
This is the tracking issue for a comprehensive contribution bringing OVHcloud VPS coverage in `terraform-provider-ovh` from ~15% to ~99% of the public API, on both EU and US regions.

The implementation PR will reference this issue. Filing this first so all related work is linked in one place.

### Closes / supersedes

This work addresses several open issues:

* Closes #1125 — `[DOCS] Clarification on public_ssh_key usage`
* Closes #1198 — `[DOCS] Clarification on image_id, plan_code, and VPS creation workflow`
* Closes #1229 — `[BUG] Seems impossible to automate proper VPS creation with ssh keys`
* Closes #943 — `[NEW] Resource: ovh_vps_reinstall`
* Functionally supersedes #1268 — `feat(VPS): add data sources for VPS images` (stalled draft, idle since 2026-03)

### Scope summary

* **20 new resources** + 1 cross-product (`ovh_vrack_vps`) covering snapshot, automated backup, backup FTP, IP reverse DNS, secondary DNS, disk monitoring, Veeam restore, console URL, VNC, migration, service info, contact change, power actions, and legacy reinstall.
* **40 new data sources** + 2 cross-product (`ovh_vrack_vps`, `ovh_vrack_vpss`) covering image discovery, catalog, tasks, options, IP/status, disks, backup families, secondary DNS, distribution & templates.
* **Bug fixes** to existing `r/ovh_vps` and `d/ovh_vps`: `sla_monitoring` create gap, missing `display_name` validator, struct field typo, offer-type map key, missing model fields exposure.

### Validation

Live-tested against `api.us.ovhcloud.com` with a `GET /*`-scoped read-only credential on a real US account: **15 PASS / 0 FAIL / 31 SKIP** on data-source acceptance tests. `TestProvider` passes under race detector. `terraform validate` against an 18-block megaconfig: `Success! The configuration is valid`.

### PR

Tracking PR: will be linked here once opened. The PR description will repeat the manifest in full.

Note for reviewers: I'm submitting this as a **single PR** to tell the complete validated story. The PR body pre-emptively offers a 12-way split if reviewers prefer smaller chunks.

title: [NEW] Data sources: ovh_vps_available_images / ovh_vps_available_image / ovh_vps_current_image
author: 88plug
body:
### Description

Three new data sources to surface VPS image metadata, closing the long-standing chicken-and-egg user issue where `image_id` is required to set `public_ssh_key` on `ovh_vps`, but `image_id` is only obtainable from `GET /vps/{serviceName}/images/available` — i.e. after the VPS already exists.

This functionally supersedes the stalled draft #1268 (idle since 2026-03, no tests, no maintainer review).

### Proposed data sources

* `ovh_vps_available_images` — wraps `GET /vps/{sn}/images/available`. Lists every image ID available for a VPS. Optional `name_pattern` regex filter on the name field. Bonus `images` list of `{id, name}` populated via a small worker-pool of per-image GETs.
* `ovh_vps_available_image` — wraps `GET /vps/{sn}/images/available/{id}`. Single image detail.
* `ovh_vps_current_image` — wraps `GET /vps/{sn}/images/current`. The image currently installed on the VPS.

### Why

This unblocks at least the following open issues:
* #1125 (docs/clarification on `public_ssh_key` requiring `image_id`)
* #1229 (impossible to automate VPS creation with ssh keys)
* #1198 (DOCS: image_id, plan_code, and VPS creation workflow)

### PR

Tracking PR is at #NNNN (will edit once filed).
